_meta.source: Aggregated from ESPN, Yahoo Sports, SI, NBC Sports, RotoWire, Covers.com

duke: Caleb Foster (starting guard) out with a foot fracture suffered in the regular-season finale vs UNC, likely done for the season unless Duke makes a deep run. Patrick Ngongba II (10.7 PPG, 6.0 RPG) is very unlikely to play in the first round but could return later in the tournament.
louisville: Mikel Brown Jr. (18.2 PPG, 4.7 APG), the team's second-leading scorer and primary playmaker, is out for the first weekend of the tournament with a back injury. He has not played since Feb. 28.
alabama: Aden Holloway (16.8 PPG, 3.8 APG), the team's second-leading scorer, was arrested for felony marijuana possession. Head coach Nate Oats has stated the team is preparing to play without him.
ucla: Tyler Bilodeau (key forward) suffered a mild right knee strain during the Big Ten quarterfinal on March 13. Expected to be available but his effectiveness is a question mark. Donovan Dent is dealing with a calf injury but is expected to play.
unc: Caleb Wilson (19.8 PPG, 9.4 RPG, 2.7 APG), the team's leading scorer and offensive engine, is out for the season with a fractured hand that required surgery. Massive loss.
byu: Richie Saunders (key wing, part of their big three with AJ Dybantsa) tore his ACL in mid-February and is out for the season. BYU has gone under .500 since his injury, losing four of their final six regular-season games.
texas-tech: JT Toppin (21.8 PPG, 10.8 RPG), the team's best player and a nightly double-double threat, suffered a season-ending knee injury on Feb. 17. Massive loss that Texas Tech cannot replace with one player.
gonzaga: Braden Huff (6-10 junior forward) has been out since January 15 with a knee injury. Gonzaga has adjusted but is missing interior depth.
kentucky: Quaintance (key player) is not expected to be ready to return in time for Kentucky's opening round game vs Santa Clara.
